extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won a hectic sprint after INEOS disrupted positioning by sprinting the second intermediate sprint with 12km remaining, forcing positional chaos.
tactical_patterns: INEOS deliberately contested second intermediate sprint 12km out, forcing teams to reposition and causing late-race chaos benefiting opportunistic sprinters.
BikeExchange committed chase resources despite BISEGGER's late advantage being under 1 minute with 35km remaining, revealing conservative gap management.
SAGAN's team controlled all day despite nine-month win drought, demonstrating faith in fading sprinters pays when chaos disrupts pure-speed trains.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: first win in nine months suggests late-career resurgence when chaos disrupts organized sprint trains.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: sprinted intermediate for time bonuses, showing GC ambition rather than domestique role at Ineos.
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: negative: caught in late crash chaos outside 3km safe zone, dropped from second to outside top ten.
rider_signals.WILLIAMS Stephen: positive: held yellow through chaotic finale demonstrating bike-handling superiority over pure climbers in technical finishes.
rider_signals.BISEGGER Stefan: neutral: repeated late attacks from break showed commitment but 59-second max gap with 35km suggests poor breakaway energy allocation.
